---
title: "Additional Resources: Data Repositories"
editor: 
  mode: source
---

This page lists data repositories and products that can support environmental data science projects. For this course, use it as a final-project planning guide: start with the response variable you want to model, then identify climate, vegetation, soil, land-cover, wetland-area, and validation data that match your study region and time period.

This list builds from resources shared by the LTER Network's [Synthesis Skills for Early Career Researchers course](https://lter.github.io/ssecr/) and [Ecological Data Synthesis: A Primer on Essential Methods course](https://lter.github.io/eco-data-synth-primer/).

## Start with your final project need

| Final project need | Good places to start | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| methane, carbon, water, or energy fluxes | [AmeriFlux](https://ameriflux.lbl.gov/data/data-policy/), [FLUXNET](https://fluxnet.org/data/fluxnet2015-dataset/), [NEON](https://data.neonscience.org/) | Check variable names, timestamps, gap filling, units, and site metadata. |
| daily point climate | [Daymet](https://daymet.ornl.gov/) | Useful for U.S. and North American site climate summaries. |
| global monthly climate or reanalysis | [ERA5](https://cds.climate.copernicus.eu/), [NOAA](https://data.noaa.gov/onestop/) | ERA5 works well for gridded predictor rasters but requires careful unit conversion. |
| vegetation index or land cover | [NASA AppEEARS](https://appeears.earthdatacloud.nasa.gov/), [Google Earth Engine](https://earthengine.google.com/), [Microsoft Planetary Computer](https://planetarycomputer.microsoft.com/) | Useful for NDVI, EVI, MODIS land cover, and other remote sensing products. |
| wetland or inundation area | [WAD2M](https://doi.org/10.5194/essd-13-2001-2021), [NASA](https://data.nasa.gov/) | Needed when converting methane flux density to emissions. |
| soils, elevation, hydrology, or disturbance | [USGS](https://www.usgs.gov/products/data/all-data), [EES-DIVE](https://ess-dive.lbl.gov/), [NEON](https://data.neonscience.org/) | Useful for static site predictors and interpretation. |
| ecological datasets with metadata | [EDI](https://edirepository.org/), [KNB](https://knb.ecoinformatics.org/), [DataONE](https://www.dataone.org/) | Good sources for reusable ecological datasets and examples of published metadata. |
| biodiversity or trait context | [GBIF](https://www.gbif.org/), [Open Traits Network](https://opentraits.org/datasets.html) | Useful when projects include species distributions, plant traits, or ecosystem context. |

## Repository table

| Name | Best use in this course | R package or access route |
|:---|:---|:---|
| [AmeriFlux](https://ameriflux.lbl.gov/data/data-policy/) | Tower flux observations, site metadata, and variable conventions for carbon, water, energy, and methane studies. | [`amerifluxr`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/amerifluxr/index.html) |
| [FLUXNET](https://fluxnet.org/data/fluxnet2015-dataset/) | Global flux tower data and background for cross-site modeling. | website download |
| [NEON](https://data.neonscience.org/) | U.S. ecological observations, meteorology, remote sensing, and site metadata. | [`neonUtilities`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/neonUtilities/index.html) |
| [Daymet](https://daymet.ornl.gov/) | Daily gridded weather for North America, especially point-based climate summaries. | [`daymetr`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/daymetr/index.html) |
| [ERA5 Climate Data Store](https://cds.climate.copernicus.eu/) | Global reanalysis predictors such as precipitation, temperature, and vapor pressure variables. | [`ecmwfr`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/ecmwfr/index.html) |
| [NASA AppEEARS](https://appeears.earthdatacloud.nasa.gov/) | Point or area downloads for MODIS, VIIRS, vegetation indices, land cover, and other remote-sensing products. | website download |
| [NASA Open Data](https://data.nasa.gov/) | NASA-hosted environmental data products, including examples of methane upscaling products. | website/API |
| [WAD2M](https://doi.org/10.5194/essd-13-2001-2021) | Monthly wetland/inundation fraction for scaling methane flux density to emissions. | data product download |
| [Google Earth Engine](https://earthengine.google.com/) | Cloud analysis of satellite imagery and large gridded datasets. | [`rgee`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/rgee/index.html) |
| [Microsoft Planetary Computer](https://planetarycomputer.microsoft.com/) | Cloud-hosted environmental and remote-sensing datasets. | [`rstac`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/rstac/index.html) |
| [EDI](https://edirepository.org/) | Long-term ecological datasets and examples of well-documented data packages. | [`EDIutils`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/EDIutils/index.html) |
| [KNB](https://knb.ecoinformatics.org/) | Ecological and environmental data packages with metadata and DOIs. | [`dataone`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/dataone/index.html) |
| [DataONE](https://www.dataone.org/) | Search across many environmental data repositories. | [`dataone`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/dataone/index.html) |
| [EES-DIVE](https://ess-dive.lbl.gov/) | Environmental system science datasets, model inputs, and data products. | website/API |
| [GBIF](https://www.gbif.org/) | Species occurrence and biodiversity records. | [`rgbif`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/rgbif/index.html) |
| [Open Traits Network](https://opentraits.org/datasets.html) | Trait dataset inventory and links to trait repositories. | varies |
| [PhenoCam Network](https://phenocam.nau.edu/webcam/) | Camera-derived vegetation phenology data. | [`phenocamapi`](https://github.com/PhenoCamNetwork/phenocamapi) |
| [NOAA OneStop](https://data.noaa.gov/onestop/) | Weather, climate, oceanographic, and atmospheric data. | varies |
| [USGS](https://www.usgs.gov/products/data/all-data) | Hydrology, geology, topography, land cover, and other environmental data. | [`dataRetrieval`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/dataRetrieval/index.html) |
| [US National Park Service](https://www.nps.gov/subjects/science/science-data.htm) | Park boundaries, vegetation, geology, air quality, and water quality data. | [`NPSutils`](https://github.com/nationalparkservice/NPSutils/) |

## Before using a dataset

For each dataset you plan to use, record:

1.  The citation or URL.
2.  The variable names and units.
3.  The spatial resolution and coordinate reference system.
4.  The temporal resolution and years available.
5.  Whether the data are raw, gap-filled, modeled, or remotely sensed.
6.  The license or data-use policy.
7.  Any processing or unit conversion needed before modeling.

This table becomes part of your final project methods section.
